module.name: pinstore
module.spec: FileStateStore.Load reads .drift/state.xml via the caller-provided fileio.Session (see fileio.session), unmarshals XML into Specs, Markers, and Edges, and returns the State. State.xml version=4 is the provenance-closure format: baseline only, no <edgeResolutions>. Pre-v4 files are refused with ErrStateVersionUnsupported, directing the user to delete .drift/ and re-init. If the file does not exist, it returns ErrStateNotFound.

FileStateStore.Save marshals State to indented XML and writes it to .drift/state.xml via the caller-provided fileio.Session (see fileio.write_atomicity), which atomically replaces any existing file via temp-file + rename. The XML root element is drift (version 4) with nested specs, markers, and edges sections. Save does not create directories or take locks; the Session owns both concerns.
When .drift/state.xml does not exist on disk, Load returns ErrStateNotFound, prompting the user to run drift init first.

BaselineStore manages content-addressed baseline files in .drift/baselines/. Each file is named by its hash. For markers, the hash is sha1(content). For specs, the hash is the canonical hash (sha1 of spec content with <ref> tags stripped, see scanner.spec_discovery); the file content is the raw innerxml including refs so that drift diff and drift show display faithful spec text. Write stores content at <hash> if absent (dedup) and does NOT verify sha1(content)==hash for the spec case. Read returns content for <hash> or false if missing. Delete removes a single file. Missing files are not errors. See principles.specs_are_truth — content-addressed by canonical hash.
